free_text: MAIN REF.
-CUMULATIVE FISSION PRODUCT YIELDS FROM MONOENERGETIC
NEUTRON INDUCED FISSION IN 238-U.
THE DYNAMITRON ACCELERATOR OF THE PHYSICS DE-
PARTMENT RADIATION CENTRE, UNIVERSITY OF BIRMINGHAM,
WAS USED FOR THE IRRADIATIONS, WHICH TOOK PLACE IN A
LOW SCATTER ROOM. THE PROTON OR DEUTERON BEAM CURRENTS
WERE UP TO 1 MILLI-AMP.
NEUTRONS WERE PRODUCED WITH THE P-T OR D-D
REACTIONS, USING SOLID TARGETS OF T OR D AB-
SORBED IN 25.4 MM DIAMETER DEPOSITS OF TI ON SILVER
BACKINGS, 0.5 - 0.75 MM THICK AND 31 MM DIAMETER.
THE SILVER BACKINGS WERE COOLED WITH A 0.5 MM THICK
STREAM OF WATER. THE BEAM WAS SWEPT TO A UNIFORM BEAM
SPOT OF 11 MM DIAMETER.
THE NEUTRON FLUENCE WAS DETERMINED BY A FISSION
CHAMBER, WITH ITS 63.7 +/- 1.3 MICRO-G DEPOSIT OF
99.99 PERCENT 238-U POSITIONED IMMEDIATELY EFTER THE
SAMPLE STACK. THE DETECTOR WAS A STANDARD PARALLEL-
PLATE IONIZATION CHAMBER MADE FROM 1 MM THICK STAIN-
LESS STEEL AND WITH A PLATE SEPARATION OF 9.5 MM. THE
238-U DEPOSIT HAD THE SAME DIAMETER AS THE SAMPLES,
AND WAS PAINTED ONTO THE INSIDE SURFACE OF THE 0.127
MM THICK PLATINUM CHAMBER WALL.
THE VARIATION OF THE NEUTRON FLUX DURING THE
SHORT, 20 MIN - 3 H, AND LONG, 6 - 24 H, IRRADIATIONS
WAS MONITORED BY USING A SECOND SMALL PARALLEL-PLATE
FISSION CHAMBER.
THE GAMMA-RAY ACTIVITIES WERE COUNTED WITH A
93 CM3 HYPERPURE GERMANIUM DETECTOR WITH A RESOLUTION
OF 1.8 KEV AT 1.33 MEV. THE DETECTOR WAS SHIELDED
WITH 5 CM THICK LEAD BRICKS, AND THE SAMPLES WERE
MOUNTED AT A DISTANCE OF 4.8 CM FROM THE FRONT FACE.
THE SYSTEM WAS ENERGY AND EFFICIENCY CALIBRATED WITH
A 152-EU SOURCE AND WITH STANDARD CALIBRATED SOURCES,
RESPECTIVELY.
THE 238-U FISSION CROSS SECTION
WAS USED FOR ABSOLUTE NEUTRON FLUENCE DETERMINATION.
.IN ADDITION, THE NEUTRON ACTIVATION CROSS SECTION OF
INDIUM WAS USED IN THE EVENT OF FISSION CHAMBER
FAILURE.
.THE SAMPLES WERE DISCS OF NATURAL URANIUM, CONTAINING
99.78 PERCENT 238-U. THEY WERE 10 MM IN DIAMETER AND
0.178 MM THICK. DURING IRRADIATIONS THEY WERE PLACED
ABOUT 20 - 30 MM FROM THE NEUTRON SOURCE. THE U DISCS
WERE SANDWICHED BETWEEN 10 MICRO-M ALUMINIUM CATCHER
FOILS TO RETAIN THE FISSION PRODUCTS. 75 MICRO-M FOILS
OF INDIUM, PLACED ON BOTH SIDES OF THE SAMPLES, SERVED
AS SECONDARY FLUENCE MONITORS. THE WHOLE MULTILAYER
PACKS WERE WRAPPED IN 10 MICRO-M ALUMINIUM FOILS. THE
PACKS WERE FINALLY COVERED WITH A SEPARATE CADMIUM
SHEET TO PREVENT THERMAL NEUTRONS TO REACH THE U DISC.
.THE ACTIVITY COUNT RATES WERE CORRECTED FOR CASCADE
COINCIDENCE SUMMING AND FOR SELF-ABSORPTION OF GAMMA-
RAYS IN THE U DISCS. THE FISSION YIELD DATA WERE
CORRECTED FOR VARIATIONS OF THE NEUTRON FLUX DURING
IRRADIATIONS AND ALLOWANCE WAS MADE FOR THE DECAY OF
PRECURSORS OF THE NUCLIDE UNDER CONSIDERATION.
THE ERRORS GIVEN INCLUDE
1. UNCERTAINTIES OF THE ABSOLUTE GAMMA-RAY EMISSION
INTENSITIES,
2. 10 PERCENT UNCERTAINTY OF THE GE-DETECTOR
EFFICIENCY CALIBRATION,
3. 2 PERCENT UNCERTAINTY OF THE FISSION CHAMBER DETEC-
TION EFFICIENCY OR 6 PERCENT UNCERTAINTY IN THE
CASE OF THE ACTIVATION FOILS.
4. UNCERTAINTIES IN THE GAMMA-PEAK AREAS.
.DATA TAKEN FROM MAIN REF.
N.O.
.DATA TAKEN FROM MAIN REF.
N.O.
